pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: Gunner is the only one who seems to have a clue in this pool, and that's a low bar when "Bubba G" barely edges through with a lucky France win. While Devin's clutching to Spain's coattails, Mustard Boy Zach seems to have drafted with his eyes closed, thinking picking Brazil alone would carry him—newsflash, pal, it won't. Gayden, or "The Backpass Assassin,” is so far out of his depth that England might as well be playing with 10 men. But the real comedy here is Rafe, "The Noisemaker," who picked three teams that didn't even qualify—it's like he thought FIFA was an arcade game. If you want to watch true managerial incompetence, look no further than Rafe, whose mere presence in this pool is an insult to everyone who actually watches soccer.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner Tops Fantasy Standings, Credits Strategy of Avoiding Upsets Entirely
pundit_takes.subtitle: With 28 points, Bubba G prefers predictability over surprise victories.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's England Poised to Validate High Draft Status Against Panama
pundit_takes.subtitle: Confidence remains high despite drafting three non-qualifiers.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Austria Set to Face Algeria With Impeccable Timing
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy banking on Austria's prime time slot fortunes.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Poised to Shake Pool as Congo Meets Uzbekistan
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker's chaos theory tested as DR Congo takes the field.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner skyrocketed to 1st, leaving a cloud of competition dust behind him.
Rafe picked non-qualifiers like a pro and still hangs with the big boys.
Rafe owns Devin; head-to-head he's Devin's personal nightmare, sweeping him clean.
Sim, you lost your magic touch: Rafe's chaos broke your crystal ball.
